initial_program:
% ---------- grid dimensions ----------
row(R) :- R = 0..9.
col(C) :- C = 0..9.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ---------- central row (row with all 5s) ----------
centreRow(R0) :- row(R0), #count{C : input(R0, C, V), V != 5} = 0.
% the puzzle guarantees a unique such row

% exactly one colour for each output cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% copy every nonzero input cell (keeps it unchanged)
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ---- colour 2 ----
% 2 above the centre  propagate downwards (but never cross the centre row)
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), output(R1,C,2), R1 < R0, R2 = R1 + 1, R2 < R0, input(R2,C,0).
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), input(R1,C,2), R1 < R0, R2 = R1 + 1, R2 < R0, input(R2,C,0).

% 2 below the centre  propagate upwards, but only if there is no 2 above the centre
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), input(R1,C,2), R1 > R0, R2 = R1 - 1, R2 > R0 + 2,
                    input(R2,C,0), #count{R : input(R, C, 2), R < R0} = 0.
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), output(R1,C,2), R1 > R0, R2 = R1 - 1, R2 > R0 + 2,
                    input(R2,C,0), #count{R : input(R, C, 2), R < R0} = 0.

% ---- colour 1 ----
% colour1 moves away from the centre (only one step downwards for rows below the centre)
output(R2,C,1) :- centreRow(R0), input(R1,C,1), R1 > R0, R2 = R1 + 1, R2 < 10, input(R2,C,0).

% ---- zero cells ----------
output(R,C,0) :- output_cell(R,C), not output(R,C,W), W != 0.

% exactly one colour per cell (already enforced by the cardinality constraint above)
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% ---------- grid dimensions ----------
row(R) :- R = 0..9.
col(C) :- C = 0..9.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ---------- central row (row with all 5s) ----------
centreRow(R0) :- row(R0), #count{C : input(R0, C, V), V != 5} = 0.
% the puzzle guarantees a unique such row

% exactly one colour for each output cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% copy every nonzero input cell (keeps it unchanged)
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ---- colour 2 ----
% 2 above the centre  propagate downwards (but never cross the centre row)
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), output(R1,C,2), R1 < R0, R2 = R1 + 1, R2 < R0, input(R2,C,0).
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), input(R1,C,2), R1 < R0, R2 = R1 + 1, R2 < R0, input(R2,C,0).

% 2 below the centre  propagate upwards, but only if there is no 2 above the centre
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), input(R1,C,2), R1 > R0, R2 = R1 - 1, R2 > R0 + 2,
                    input(R2,C,0), #count{R : input(R, C, 2), R < R0} = 0.
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), output(R1,C,2), R1 > R0, R2 = R1 - 1, R2 > R0 + 2,
                    input(R2,C,0), #count{R : input(R, C, 2), R < R0} = 0.

% ---- colour 1 ----
% colour1 moves away from the centre (only one step downwards for rows below the centre)
output(R2,C,1) :- centreRow(R0), input(R1,C,1), R1 > R0, R2 = R1 + 1, R2 < 10, input(R2,C,0).

% ---- zero cells ----------
output(R,C,0) :- output_cell(R,C), not output(R,C,W), W != 0.

% exactly one colour per cell (already enforced by the cardinality constraint above)
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

row(R) :- R = 0..9.
col(C) :- C = 0..9.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ---------- central row (row with all 5s) ----------
centreRow(R0) :- row(R0), #count{C : input(R0, C, V), V != 5} = 0.
% the puzzle guarantees a unique such row

% exactly one colour for each output cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% copy every nonzero input cell (keeps it unchanged)
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ---- colour 2 ----
% 2 above the centre  propagate downwards (but never cross the centre row)
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), output(R1,C,2), R1 < R0, R2 = R1 + 1, R2 < R0, input(R2,C,0).
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), input(R1,C,2), R1 < R0, R2 = R1 + 1, R2 < R0, input(R2,C,0).

% 2 below the centre  propagate upwards, but only if there is no 2 above the centre
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), input(R1,C,2), R1 > R0, R2 = R1 - 1, R2 > R0 + 2,
                    input(R2,C,0), #count{R : input(R, C, 2), R < R0} = 0.
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), output(R1,C,2), R1 > R0, R2 = R1 - 1, R2 > R0 + 2,
                    input(R2,C,0), #count{R : input(R, C, 2), R < R0} = 0.

% ---- colour 1 ----
% colour1 moves away from the centre (only one step downwards for rows below the centre)
output(R2,C,1) :- centreRow(R0), input(R1,C,1), R1 > R0, R2 = R1 + 1, R2 < 10, input(R2,C,0).

% ---- zero cells ----------
output(R,C,0) :- output_cell(R,C), color(W), not output(R,C,W).

% exactly one colour per cell (already enforced by the cardinality constraint above)
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

final_program:
% ---------- grid dimensions ----------
row(R) :- R = 0..9.
col(C) :- C = 0..9.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ---------- central row (row with all 5s) ----------
centreRow(R0) :- row(R0), #count{C : input(R0, C, V), V != 5} = 0.
% the puzzle guarantees a unique such row

% exactly one colour for each output cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% copy every nonzero input cell (keeps it unchanged)
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ---- colour 2 ----
% 2 above the centre  propagate downwards (but never cross the centre row)
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), output(R1,C,2), R1 < R0, R2 = R1 + 1, R2 < R0, input(R2,C,0).
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), input(R1,C,2), R1 < R0, R2 = R1 + 1, R2 < R0, input(R2,C,0).

% 2 below the centre  propagate upwards, but only if there is no 2 above the centre
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), input(R1,C,2), R1 > R0, R2 = R1 - 1, R2 > R0 + 2,
                    input(R2,C,0), #count{R : input(R, C, 2), R < R0} = 0.
output(R2,C,2) :- centreRow(R0), output(R1,C,2), R1 > R0, R2 = R1 - 1, R2 > R0 + 2,
                    input(R2,C,0), #count{R : input(R, C, 2), R < R0} = 0.

% ---- colour 1 ----
% colour1 moves away from the centre (only one step downwards for rows below the centre)
output(R2,C,1) :- centreRow(R0), input(R1,C,1), R1 > R0, R2 = R1 + 1, R2 < 10, input(R2,C,0).

% ---- zero cells ----------
output(R,C,0) :- output_cell(R,C), color(W), not output(R,C,W).

% exactly one colour per cell (already enforced by the cardinality constraint above)
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.
